question: Define a C++ `struct` named `Book` with members `title` (std::string), `author` (std::string), and `yearPublished` (int). Write a function `printBookDetails` that takes a `Book` struct (by value) and prints all its details. Demonstrate by creating a `Book` instance and calling the function.

test_cases.expected:
Title: To Kill a Mockingbird
Author: Harper Lee
Year: 1960

driver: int main() { Book myBook; myBook.title = "To Kill a Mockingbird"; myBook.author = "Harper Lee"; myBook.yearPublished = 1960; printBookDetails(myBook); return 0; }
errors: The printBookDetails function incorrectly prints the author's name instead of the yearPublished for the 'Year' field.
